input.title: Inventory and Warehouse Management System
input.description: Inventory and Warehouse Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Product Catalog Management
requirements: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category

FR-03.title: Purchase Order Management
requirements: Track PO status (draft, sent, received, completed)

FR-04.title: Sales Order Processing
requirements: Create sales orders from customer requests

FR-05.title: Warehouse Zone Management
requirements: Define warehouse zones and bin locations

FR-08.title: Barcode Scanning
requirements: Scan barcodes for quick product lookup
Scan for receiving, picking, and counting

FR-09.title: Stock Counting and Reconciliation
requirements: Count entry by zone or full warehouse
Variance report comparing system vs physical

FR-10.title: Reporting and Analytics
requirements: Stock valuation report (FIFO, LIFO, weighted average)

FR-01.title: Design Product Catalog Management UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for product catalog management. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for product catalog management is complete and reviewable.
The product catalog management design is responsive and accessible.
The design for product catalog management aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Product Catalog Management
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for product catalog management. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category; Product variants (size, color, material); Barcode and QR code generation.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required product catalog management user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for product catalog management are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for product catalog management is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Product Catalog Management
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for product catalog management. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category; Product variants (size, color, material); Barcode and QR code generation.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for product catalog management is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for product catalog management enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for product catalog management is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Product Catalog Management
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for product catalog management. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for product catalog management supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for product catalog management are defined correctly.
Schema changes for product catalog management can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Product Catalog Management
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for product catalog management. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for product catalog management enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for product catalog management are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of product catalog management satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Testing Product Catalog Management Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for product catalog management. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for product catalog management.
Security, validation, and permission checks for product catalog management are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important product catalog management behaviors.
